title: Exposure assessment foundation for occupational health hazards
standardIntent: Support recognition of occupational health exposure pathways, sampling needs, uncertainty, and health-risk context. This record is advisory and does not create legal compliance requirements by itself.
evidenceNeeded: Document exposure route, task, duration, frequency, and affected employees.
Collect quantitative sampling data when exposure compliance or dose estimation is needed.
Document controls such as ventilation, wet methods, enclosure, respiratory protection, and administrative controls.
Record uncertainty when only qualitative exposure information is available.
nonApplicabilityQuestions: Is there an identifiable health agent or exposure route?
Is quantitative sampling required before making a compliance or exposure-limit statement?
Is the source guidance rather than a binding regulation?
Are OSHA, MSHA, or other enforceable exposure limits more directly applicable?
